untranslatable_reason: Box spread arbitrage requires multi-leg options pricing analysis (theoretical box value calculation, call vertical spread pricing, put vertical spread pricing, combined leg pricing, and deviation thresholds). These are derivatives-specific calculations that depend on strike prices, implied volatility surfaces, and simultaneous execution across two separate spreads. The allowed indicators are limited to single-instrument price and technical analysis metrics. Box spread valuation requires: (1) access to multiple option chain prices at different strikes, (2) present value discounting calculations, (3) bid-ask spread data across multiple legs, and (4) execution synchronization logic—none of which can be expressed using the allowed price, moving average, RSI, ATR, Bollinger, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, pivot, volume, or position indicators.
translation_notes: This signal describes a volatility arbitrage strategy specific to options markets requiring real-time multi-leg option pricing and coordinated execution. It cannot be translated into a single-instrument backtesting rule using technical indicators.
